FREE: 10 Posts that Generated 1 MILLION Impressions & 900+ Leads. Click “Visit my website” to get them.
content:
Not every idea is a good idea.


Most people treat LinkedIn like a braindump. Every thought that passes through their head becomes a post. 

Something they read this morning. 
A quote they liked. 
A vague observation about their industry.

And they wonder why nothing works.

The problem isn't consistency. Posting every day with content that connects to nobody is just noise with a schedule.

The posts that actually work are the ones you sit with for a minute before publishing. 

Not because they took longer to write. Because they started with a specific person in mind. 

Someone you know, 
someone you've talked to, 
someone who has a problem you can actually solve.

When you write for that person, the post stops feeling like content and starts feeling like a conversation. 

And conversations are what turn followers into leads.

Most people have the ratio backwards. 

They spend 80% of their time coming up with ideas and 20% thinking about who they're writing for. Flip that and everything changes.

The idea is rarely the problem. What you do with it is.

I created 10 posts that were written for one specific person and they generated 900+ leads and over 1 MILLION impressions.

I packaged them into one free file with proof of results and why each one worked.

If you are interesed, you can check them here: https://lnkd.in/dcu3arA4


P.S. The best post you'll write this month probably isn't an idea you came up with. It's a question someone asked you last week that you haven't written down yet.

content:
I Generated 400+ Leads in 30 Days!

Zero cold outreach. Just content.

When I started posting on LinkedIn, I made the same mistake everyone makes.

I posted whenever I had time.
I shared what felt "interesting."
I hoped something would work.

And I got maybe 5-10 leads per month, on a good month.

Until I realized...

It's not about posting more, it's about posting strategically.

I discovered that these 5 post types consistently drove leads:

→ Posts that solve expensive problems (not tips)
→ Posts with proof (screenshots, not promises)
→ Posts written for one specific person (not "all founders")
→ Posts with one clear CTA (not multiple)
→ Posts that are honest (not hype)

But here's the thing...

Knowing the types isn't enough. You need to see the actual posts.

The exact hooks.
The structure.
The CTAs that convert.

That's why I compiled my top 10 highest-performing posts:

✓ Generated 900+ leads total
✓ Over 1 MILLION impressions combined
✓ Each one includes a breakdown of why it worked

These aren't templates. They're real posts with real leads generated.

Most people post on LinkedIn...and hope for leads.

These 10 posts? They're proven to convert.

Want all of them (plus the breakdowns)?

Get them here, it's FREE: https://lnkd.in/dXevs7Fq

authorHeadline:

FREE: 10 Posts that Generated 1 MILLION Impressions & 900+ Leads. Click “Visit my website” to get them.
content:
If you are new to LinkedIn, here's what your first 5 posts should be...

I wish someone had told me this when I started. Would have saved me months of posting for nothing, getting 4 likes and questioning my entire existence.

So, these 5 posts should be your first if you never posted before:


Post 1: Who you are and why you're here.

Not your CV but more like one paragraph. Why now, why LinkedIn, what you'll be talking about. People follow people, not resumes.


Post 2: A mistake you made in your field.

Here what we want is an actual mistake. What went wrong, what you felt, what you learned. 

This one post will get you more followers than 10 "tips" posts ever will.

People love when others suffered. Harsh but truth.


Post 3: Something you believe that most people in your industry don't.

Controversy doesn't mean offensive, it means having a take. Safe opinions get scrolled past.


Post 4: A moment that changed how you think about your work.

No need for a big dramatic story. It could be a conversation, a client reaction. Something someone said that you still think about.


Post 5: Something you wish you knew when you started.


The easiest post you'll ever write. Almost always the one that blows up.

That's your first month on LinkedIn done.

And if you want to see exactly how posts like these turn into leads, I packaged my 10 posts into one free file.

These posts got me:

1 MILLION impressions
900+ leads

In this file I also explain why each of them worked and how you can use them for your industry.

You can get all 10 of them here: https://lnkd.in/djr5KvyU

authorHeadline:

FREE: 10 Posts that Generated 1 MILLION Impressions & 900+ Leads. Click “Visit my website” to get them.
content:
Want to tell a story that actually sells?

4 things you need:

1. A hook

The first line either stops someone or it doesn't. There's no in between. 

If they don't read your first line, they won't read the rest.

2. Make it relatable

People only care about stories they can see themselves in.

3. Show, don't just state facts

Saying "it was hard" means nothing. Describe the moment, make them feel it.

4. End with a takeaway

And never just… end. Leave people with something. 

One thought they didn't have before they started reading. That's what gets saved, shared and remembered.

That's it. 4 things to a story that sells.

I used these exact four things across 10 posts that generated 1 MILLION impressions and 900+ leads.

I packaged all 10 into a free file.

the exact wording,
the moment that inspired each one, 
and why it worked.

You can get them here for free: https://lnkd.in/dqpTt3zy

authorHeadline:
FREE: 10 Posts that Generated 1 MILLION Impressions & 900+ Leads. Click “Visit my website” to get them.
FREE: 10 Posts that Generated 1 MILLION Impressions & 900+ Leads. Click “Visit my website” to get them.
content:
Two posts. 80,000 impressions in a week.

Here's exactly why they worked...

Neither of them was a long breakdown. 
Neither had a list of tips. 
Neither started with "I've been thinking about..."

The first one: "VW's competitors said their car was too small. So VW made an ad proving they were right."

The second one: "One of these videos has 18M views. Can you guess which one?"

Both made people stop and read for the same reason. They opened a loop the brain couldn't close without clicking.

The VW post worked because it set up a contradiction. 

Most companies fight back when someone says their product has a flaw. VW agreed. That's so counterintuitive that you have to know what happened next.

The video post worked because it turned passive reading into a game. 

The moment you see four options and a number like 18 million, something in your brain needs to know if it guessed right. You're not reading anymore. You're competing.

Both posts also did something most LinkedIn content never does...

They made commenting feel natural. 

On the VW post people wanted to share their take on the lesson. On the video post they wanted to guess. 

The engagement wasn't forced. The format created it.

I spent a year writing posts that explained, then I switched to posts that made people feel something or need to know something. 

And from that I collected 10 that worked and I packaged them into one free file.

These are results:
• 1 MILLION impressions.
• 900+ leads.

Btw, also in that file, I breakdown of what made each one work.

You can get all 10 posts here for free: https://lnkd.in/d6T4Ewmg


P.S. Go look at your last 5 posts. Did any of them make someone need to know what comes next?
